name: Texas Barbecued Brisket
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons ground red New Mexican chile
2 tablespoons ground red New Mexican chile
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the wood chips for 2 hours, then drain.
2. Prepare a slow charcoal fire in a covered grill and arrange half of the soaked wood chips on the coals.
3. Combine all dry rub ingredients except vinegar. Brush brisket with vinegar and coat thoroughly with the rub.
4. Place brisket fat side up over a drip pan on the grill. Close lid and smoke for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, adding more soaked wood chips as needed.
5. To make sauce: Sauté onion and garlic in oil until soft. Add remaining ingredients and simmer 30 minutes.
6. Generously brush brisket with sauce a couple of times during last hour of cooking.
7. Keep sauce warm and add catsup to desired consistency.
8. Cut brisket across grain and serve with sauce on the side.
